structural_claim: Spontaneous symmetry breaking generates mass and determines the vacuum structure in both quantum field theory and nuclear physics

supporting_results.structural_conclusion: Gauge symmetries can be hidden rather than broken, with massless gauge bosons acquiring mass through interaction with a scalar field whose vacuum expectation value breaks the symmetry spontaneously
The QCD vacuum spontaneously breaks chiral symmetry, generating constituent quark masses and explaining why nucleons are much heavier than the sum of their quark masses
